summary: Define LOGICAL-PATHNAME as a CLOS class (subclass of PATHNAME) so method specializers resolve, and add stub TRANSLATE-LOGICAL-PATHNAME / LOGICAL-PATHNAME-TRANSLATIONS functions.

items.title: Add LOGICAL-PATHNAME bootstrap class (subclass of PATHNAME)
items.description: In lib/clos.lisp, immediately after the existing PATHNAME bootstrap class (currently `(%make-bootstrap-class 'pathname (list (find-class 't)))` around line 272-273), add `(%make-bootstrap-class 'logical-pathname (list (find-class 'pathname)))`. This mirrors the standard CL hierarchy (LOGICAL-PATHNAME is a subtype of PATHNAME) and must come AFTER pathname so the superclass CPL already exists. The symbol LOGICAL-PATHNAME is already interned in the CL package (src/core/builtins.c reserved-symbol list), so no symbol-interning change is needed; this only registers a class metaobject in the class table via the existing %make-bootstrap-class path (which calls (setf (find-class name) class) and computes the CPL). This makes `(find-class 'logical-pathname)` succeed, which is what `%resolve-specializers` (lib/clos.lisp:1997, called from %add-method-to-gf) needs so that `(defmethod foo ((x logical-pathname)) ...)` no longer errors at load — the exact failure blocking slynk-fancy-inspector. Do NOT change builtins_type.c:233: `(typep obj 'logical-pathname)` must keep returning NIL because clamiga produces no logical pathnames (a physical pathname is not a logical pathname), and that line short-circuits before any CLOS path. Add a host regression test in tests/test_clos.c asserting: (1) `(class-name (find-class 'logical-pathname))` => "LOGICAL-PATHNAME"; (2) the direct superclass is PATHNAME via `(class-name (car (class-direct-superclasses (find-class 'logical-pathname))))` => "PATHNAME"; (3) the actual bug reproduction — defining a generic function and a method specialized on logical-pathname loads without error, e.g. `(progn (defgeneric lp-test (x)) (defmethod lp-test ((x logical-pathname)) :lp) :ok)` => ":OK". Follow the existing TEST(...) / eval_print / ASSERT_STR_EQ patterns already in tests/test_clos.c (e.g. find_class_integer near line 151), and register the new tests in the file's test runner the same way the surrounding tests are registered. Add a mirroring Amiga test to tests/amiga/run-tests.lisp using the existing `(check "name" expected form)` style (near the pathname tests around line 2140), covering find-class success, the PATHNAME superclass, and a defmethod-on-logical-pathname-loads check. Run `make test` and ensure it passes.

items.title: Define TRANSLATE-LOGICAL-PATHNAME and LOGICAL-PATHNAME-TRANSLATIONS as CL functions
items.description: Provide CL-package function bindings for the two currently-unbound symbols (lower priority; their bodies won't run since clamiga produces no logical pathnames, but they are standard CL functions worth having). Define them in lib/boot.lisp in the pathname section (after the comment block around lines 1680-1684, before ensure-directories-exist). Sensible stub semantics: (a) `(defun translate-logical-pathname (pathspec &rest keys) (declare (ignore keys)) (pathname pathspec))` — per HyperSpec it translates a logical pathname to a physical one; since clamiga has only physical pathnames, coercing via the existing PATHNAME builtin and returning it is correct (an already-physical pathname translates to itself). (b) `(defun logical-pathname-translations (host) (declare (ignore host)) nil)` — clamiga defines no logical hosts, so there are no translations; return NIL rather than erroring so introspection code (e.g. slynk) stays safe. Then, per the instruction comment at src/core/builtins.c:1228-1234 ("When a real implementation is added later it should remove the corresponding entry from this list"), remove the now-backed entries from the cl_reserved_symbol_names array: delete "LOGICAL-PATHNAME-TRANSLATIONS" (line 1266, keep "LOGICAL-PATHNAME" on that line — it stays a class/type name with no function) and "TRANSLATE-LOGICAL-PATHNAME" (line 1289). Do NOT touch "LOGICAL-PATHNAME" or "LOAD-LOGICAL-PATHNAME-TRANSLATIONS" (out of scope). Verify ASDF still loads: asdf.lisp defines its own translate-logical-pathname in the uiop package (line 1048) and the symbols at line 979 are uiop-package symbols, so a CL-package binding is independent — but confirm by running tests/test_asdf.c. Add host tests (tests/test_pathname.c) asserting: `(fboundp 'translate-logical-pathname)` => T; `(pathnamep (translate-logical-pathname #P"foo.lisp"))` => T (and that it round-trips the namestring); `(logical-pathname-translations "SYS")` => NIL. Follow the existing test/registration patterns in tests/test_pathname.c. Add mirroring `(check ...)` cases to tests/amiga/run-tests.lisp near the pathname tests (around line 2140). Run `make test` and ensure it passes.
